ALL SPECIALTIES / DENTAL ASSISTANT / OREGON

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Oregon Dental Assistant Certification

Oregon Dental Assistant license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-22 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dental Assistant applications in Oregon run through Oregon Board of Dentistry. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Proof of Training - Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
  • DANB Certification - Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
  • Radiography Certification - Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 2 to 4 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the Oregon Board of Dentistry by certification.

  1. STEP 1

    Create a Licensing Gateway Account

    Register for a new account on the Oregon Board of Dentistry's online Licensing Gateway portal to begin your application.

    Fee: $75 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Submit Initial Application

    Log in to the Licensing Gateway and select 'Initial Application' for the specific Dental Assistant certificate you are seeking (e.g., Dental Assistant Certificate, Radiology Health and Safety (RHS), Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA)).

    Fee: $75 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Provide Required Documentation

    Upload all necessary supporting documents, which may include official transcripts from approved programs, proof of out-of-state certification, verification of work experience, and/or official DANB exam results, directly through the Licensing Gateway.

    Fee: $75 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Pay Application Fee

    Remit the non-refundable application fee for your selected Dental Assistant certificate through the Licensing Gateway.

    Fee: $75 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Board Review and Approval

    The Oregon Board of Dentistry will review your complete application and all submitted documentation for compliance with state requirements.

    Fee: $75 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Receive Certificate

    Upon successful review and approval by the Board, your Dental Assistant certificate will be issued, and you can typically view and print it from your Licensing Gateway account.

    Fee: $75 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$75
Background check
Varies by provider
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$75

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Oregon Board of Dentistry
Phone
(971) 673-3200

Application page
Open application

Renewal

Dental Assistant renewal in Oregon follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Annually
Renewal fee
$75
Continuing education
24 hours per renewal cycle

Frequently Asked Questions

Does Oregon require a dental assistant license?

Yes, Oregon requires Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the state board.

Is DANB certification recognized in Oregon?

Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Oregon.

Can I take dental x-rays in Oregon?

Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Oregon?

State application fees are $75. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

Endorsement fee

$75

Processing time

2 to 4 Weeks

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

Oregon Expanded Functions Examination

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Annually · $75

Licensing board

Oregon Board of Dentistry
